varieties.name: Garden Prince
varieties.origin: USA, developed as genetic dwarf cross, commercialized 1980s-1990s
varieties.traits: Genetic dwarf, 6-9 ft tall, compact growth, large pink blossoms
varieties.yield_notes: 10-15 lbs per tree, early bearing (2-3 years)
varieties.availability: Occasionally available through specialty Australian nurseries on request, more common in southern states

varieties.name: All-In-One
varieties.origin: California, USA, modern cultivar developed mid-20th century
varieties.traits: Semi-dwarf, 10-12 ft tall, spreading form, white to pale pink blossoms
varieties.availability: Limited in Australia, mainly available in temperate regions (Victoria, South Australia)

varieties.name: Self-Pollinating (Dwarf Self-Pollinating)
varieties.origin: Australia, selection bred for backyard cultivation
varieties.traits: Semi-dwarf, 20-30% smaller than standard almonds, upright form
varieties.availability: Available through Daley's Fruit Trees (Queensland) and other Australian nurseries, though not recommended for coastal/subtropical Qld

varieties.name: Nonpareil
varieties.origin: California, 1879, discovered by A.T. Hatch in Suisun
varieties.traits: Large upright to spreading tree, vigorous growth
varieties.availability: Widely available in temperate Australian regions

varieties.name: Carmel
varieties.origin: California, modern cultivar, 20th century
varieties.traits: Slightly smaller than Nonpareil, early season variety, susceptible to blossom brown rot and scab
varieties.yield_notes: High quality nuts, late season harvest (after Price, before Mission)
varieties.availability: Commercial availability in temperate regions (30% of Australian crop)

varieties.name: Price
varieties.origin: Durham, California, 1953, chance seedling (Nonpareil × Mission cross)
varieties.traits: Medium vigor, used to cross-pollinate Nonpareil
varieties.availability: Commercial availability (11% of Australian crop)

varieties.name: Monterey
varieties.origin: California, 1974, chance seedling (apparent Nonpareil-Mission parentage)
varieties.traits: Excellent pollenizer for late Nonpareil bloom, excellent producer
varieties.availability: Available in temperate Australian regions

varieties.name: Carina
varieties.origin: University of Adelaide, released 2016, cross of Nonpareil × Lauranne
varieties.traits: Open to spreading, low-medium vigor, flowers 1-4 days before Nonpareil
varieties.yield_notes: 10%+ higher than Nonpareil, same water usage
varieties.availability: Australian nurseries (commercial release 2016)

varieties.name: Vela
varieties.origin: University of Adelaide, released 2017, cross of Chellaston × 1bT47 (Nonpareil × Lauranne)
varieties.traits: Medium vigor
varieties.availability: Australian nurseries (commercial release 2017)

varieties.name: Maxima
varieties.origin: University of Adelaide, released 2016, cross of Nonpareil × Lauranne
varieties.traits: Spreading tree, medium vigor, flowers 3-6 days after Nonpareil
varieties.availability: Australian nurseries, popular for large kernel

varieties.name: Mira, Capella, Rhea
varieties.origin: University of Adelaide, released 2016-2017
varieties.availability: Australian nurseries

varieties.name: Chellaston
varieties.origin: Australia, old established variety, pre-1900s selection
varieties.traits: Medium vigour, heavy bearer, mid-season ripening, dark skin
varieties.availability: Heritage nurseries (Yalca Fruit Trees, Heritage Fruit Trees)

varieties.name: Brandes Jordan
varieties.origin: Australia, historic selection
varieties.traits: Suitable for hot climates (within temperate range), early ripening
varieties.availability: Heritage Fruit Trees

varieties.name: Johnston's Prolific
varieties.origin: Australia, historic variety
varieties.traits: Biennial cropper, late season, nuts easily hulled
varieties.availability: Heritage Fruit Trees

varieties.name: I.X.L. (IXL)
varieties.origin: Suisun, California, 1879, by A.T. Hatch (same source as Nonpareil)
varieties.traits: Tall upright tree, vigorous, stocky twisted trunk, blooms 1 week before Nonpareil (frost risk), partially self-fertile
varieties.yield_notes: Satisfactory bearing under various conditions, thrifty and vigorous
varieties.availability: Heritage Fruit Trees, limited stock

varieties.name: Ne Plus Ultra
varieties.origin: California, 1800s, A.T. Hatch seedling
varieties.traits: Spreading tree, early bloom, susceptible to diseases similar to early bloomers
varieties.availability: Trees of Antiquity (USA), Heritage Australian nurseries, limited availability

varieties.name: Mission (Texas Prolific)
varieties.origin: Texas, USA, circa 1891, brought to California (Acampo)
varieties.traits: Vigorous when young (decreases with age), upright, easy to train, late bloom (reduced frost susceptibility), can be short-lived
varieties.yield_notes: High productivity (compensates for small kernel size)
varieties.availability: Limited in Australia

varieties.name: Peerless
varieties.origin: Near Davis, California, before 1900, unknown parentage
varieties.traits: Medium size tree, medium to upright growth, early bloom pollenizer for Nonpareil, susceptible to frost, tendency to drop nuts prematurely
varieties.availability: Declining acreage (3,703 acres in California as of 2021), rare in Australia

varieties.name: Drake, Languedoc
varieties.availability: Extremely rare, mostly found in heritage collections (Drake: 116 acres in CA; Languedoc: no longer commercially listed)

varieties.name: Mamra Badam 🏆
varieties.origin: Iran, Afghanistan, Kashmir - traditional Middle Eastern and Central Asian variety
varieties.traits: Small, round, compact size, boat-like/concave shape, rough striped texture
varieties.yield_notes: Lower commercial yields (not mass-produced), hand-harvested from individual trees
varieties.growing_notes: Cold temperate (Kashmir winters), NOT suitable for Kerala
varieties.availability: Kashmir, imported to Kerala markets from Kashmir/Afghanistan/Iran, expensive due to limited supply

varieties.name: Gurbandi Badam (Choti Giri) 🏆
varieties.origin: Afghanistan, Kashmir - traditional variety
varieties.traits: Smaller than California/Mamra (hence 'Choti Giri' = small kernel), darker color, denser
varieties.growing_notes: Cold temperate, NOT suitable for Kerala
varieties.availability: Kashmir, imported to Kerala markets from Kashmir/Afghanistan, expensive, limited supply

varieties.name: Kashmiri Badam (General) 🏆
varieties.origin: Kashmir Valley, India - encompasses various local selections
varieties.traits: Varies by tree - non-hybrid native varieties produce different shapes, sizes, flavors per tree
varieties.growing_notes: Kashmir temperate climate with cold winters, NOT suitable for Kerala 100% natural, no chemical treatment, kernel sold in shell or as 'giri' (kernels)
varieties.availability: Kashmir orchards, FSSAI/ISO certified suppliers ship to Kerala, sold in Kerala markets as premium imported product

varieties.name: Nonpareil
varieties.traits: Well-suited to subtropical and tropical climates of northern India (NOTE: This refers to the subtropical zones of Kashmir/Himachal, NOT Kerala's tropical monsoon)
varieties.availability: Kashmir/Himachal nurseries, Mahindra Nursery

varieties.name: Butte
varieties.availability: Kashmir/Himachal nurseries

varieties.name: Padre
varieties.availability: Kashmir/Himachal nurseries, Mahindra Nursery
